title: Numerically Stable Softmax
description:
Implement softmax over the last axis: $\text{softmax}(x)_i = \dfrac{e^{x_i}}{\sum_j e^{x_j}}$.

Subtract the row max before exponentiating for numerical stability. Input `x` has shape `(n, d)`; return shape `(n, d)`.
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def softmax(x):
    # TODO
    pass
hints: Subtract max along axis=-1 before exp.

solutions.name: Stable softmax
solutions.explanation: Shift by the row max, exponentiate, normalize. Softmax is invariant to a per-row additive shift since it cancels between numerator and denominator: $\frac{e^{x-k}}{\sum e^{x'-k}} = \frac{e^x}{\sum e^{x'}}$, and subtracting the max keeps every exponent $\le 0$.
